concerns.text: FR-002 mandates injecting errors at '[deferred], [deferred], [deferred], and [deferred] rates'. This contradicts the Constitution (Principle VI) which explicitly requires 'documented rates (1%, 5%, 10%, 20%)'. A requirement cannot be both 'deferred' and 'mandated' simultaneously. The spec must resolve this conflict by either adopting the specific rates from the Constitution or amending the Constitution.

User Story 1 Acceptance Scenarios 1-3 repeatedly use '[deferred]' as the specific error rate in the 'When' clause (e.g., 'When the system applies [deferred]... random value replacement'). This creates a logical impossibility in the acceptance criteria: the system cannot be tested against a specific percentage if the percentage is undefined. This conflicts with the 'Independent Test' which requires verifying 'exactly the specified percentage'.

User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 1 references 'error rates [deferred], [deferred], [deferred], [deferred]'. This is inconsistent with the requirement to generate 'degradation curves' which necessitates a defined set of x-axis values. The spec fails to define the independent variable for the visualization, making the requirement untestable as written.

The 'ErrorConfiguration' entity defines injection rates as '[deferred], [deferred], [deferred], [deferred]'. While this mirrors the FRs, it creates a circular definition where the entity's attributes are placeholders. It should reference the specific rates defined in the Constitution (1%, 5%, 10%, 20%) to ensure the data model is consistent with the operational requirements.
